question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network with invalid/rejected transactions to drive all publish scenario rates (Scenario::getRate) to zero, causing health() to return DOWN or UNKNOWN and masking real network health from operators?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit competing transactions that exhaust the operator account's HBAR balance, causing all publish scenarios to fail and driving the aggregated rate in publishing() to zero, resulting in a false DOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with submitted transactions to cause the monitor's publish rate to drop to zero while the subscribe rate remains positive, causing health() to skip the subscribing() branch and return a false UNKNOWN status that hides actual subscriber activity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user cause repeated transaction failures that flip the static CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger between 0 and 1 at high frequency, creating a race condition in recordHealthMetric() that causes Prometheus/Grafana to observe a permanently oscillating cluster health metric?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the REST API service to become overloaded, making getNetworkStakeStatusCode() return a 5xx response, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return Status.DOWN and short-circuit the entire health() evaluation without checking actual publish/subscribe rates?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a flood of requests to the /network/stake REST endpoint to trigger a timeout exceeding Duration.ofSeconds(5), causing onErrorResume() to classify the health as Status.DOWN and suppress the real publish/subscribe health evaluation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit duplicate or replayed transactions that cause the consensus node to reject them with DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION errors, driving the publish scenario rate to zero and causing publishing() to emit a DOWN/UNKNOWN health signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with submitted transactions so that the mirror node never indexes them, causing mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() to report zero rate in subscribing(), and the health indicator to return DOWN/UNKNOWN even though the network is healthy?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transaction submission failures that make publishing() emit a non-empty Mono (zero rate), preventing health() from ever calling subscribing() via switchIfEmpty(), thereby permanently hiding subscriber health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getHealthForZeroRate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all publish scenarios to fail (rate=0), and when failWhenInactive=false (default), exploit getHealthForZeroRate() returning UNKNOWN instead of DOWN to mask a real outage from alerting systems that only trigger on DOWN status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with invalid memo fields or oversized payloads that cause the TransactionGenerator scenarios to throw exceptions, resulting in scenarios being disabled and the aggregated rate in publishing() dropping to zero?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained transaction failure that keeps CLUSTER_UP set to 0 via recordHealthMetric(), causing any Prometheus alert rule watching hiero.mirror.monitor.health{type=cluster} to fire a false critical alert and trigger an incident response?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the database to become unavailable (as noted in the comment about ConnectException), triggering the onErrorResume() path in restNetworkStakeHealth() to classify health as Status.DOWN, causing a false cluster-down signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with submitted transactions to cause the publish rate to drop to exactly zero, exploiting the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) boundary condition in publishing() to trigger a health state change that would not occur at any positive rate, even 0.0001?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that are accepted by consensus but never appear in the mirror node REST/gRPC APIs, causing the subscribe rate to drop to zero in subscribing() and triggering a false DOWN/UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a targeted denial-of-service against the consensus node handling the monitor's operator account, causing all publish scenarios to stall and the rate in publishing() to drop to zero, resulting in a false DOWN health report?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restNetworkStakeHealth() uses a hard-coded 5-second timeout (Duration.ofSeconds(5)) by causing the /network/stake endpoint to respond in exactly 5+ seconds, reliably triggering TimeoutException and forcing Status.DOWN regardless of actual network health?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the monitor's operator account to be throttled by the network, reducing the publish rate to zero and causing health() to report UNKNOWN/DOWN, which could be used to trigger automated failover or incident response?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions to the same topic used by the monitor's CONSENSUS_SUBMIT_MESSAGE scenario at a rate that causes the topic to be throttled, reducing the monitor's publish rate to zero and causing publishing() to return a DOWN/UNKNOWN health signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static (class-level) CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger in recordHealthMetric() by causing concurrent health() calls to race, where one call sets CLUSTER_UP=1 and another immediately sets it to 0, creating a persistent false-down metric state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with submitted transactions to cause the publish rate to oscillate around zero, causing health() to alternate between UP and DOWN on each health check poll, creating alert fatigue that causes operators to ignore real outage alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential evaluation in health() — where restNetworkStakeHealth() must return UP before publishing()/subscribing() are checked — to cause a false DOWN by only attacking the /network/stake endpoint without needing to affect actual transaction publishing?
